root_text.an7.63:1.1: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.an7.63:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa nivesane manussā uccāsaddā mahāsaddā honti.
root_text.an7.63:1.3: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho anāthapiṇḍikaṁ gahapatiṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an7.63:2.1: “Kiṁ nu te, gahapati, nivesane manussā uccāsaddā mahāsaddā kevaṭṭā maññe macchavilope”ti?
root_text.an7.63:2.3: Sā neva sassuṁ ādiyati, na sasuraṁ ādiyati, na sāmikaṁ ādiyati, bhagavantampi na sakkaroti na garuṁ karoti na māneti na pūjetī”ti.
root_text.an7.63:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā sujātaṁ gharasuṇhaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.an7.63:3.3: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho sujātā gharasuṇhā bhagavato paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho sujātaṁ gharasuṇhaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an7.63:4.3: Vadhakasamā, corīsamā, ayyasamā, mātāsamā, bhaginīsamā, sakhīsamā, dāsīsamā.
root_text.an7.63:4.4: Imā kho, sujāte, satta purisassa bhariyāyo.
root_text.an7.63:4.6: “Na kho ahaṁ, bhante, imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi.
root_text.an7.63:4.7: Sādhu me, bhante, bhagavā tathā dhammaṁ desetu yathāhaṁ imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ jāneyyan”ti.
root_text.an7.63:4.8: “Tena hi, sujāte, suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an7.63:4.9: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho sujātā gharasuṇhā bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.an7.63:14.1: Imā kho, sujāte, satta purisassa bhariyāyo.
root_text.an7.63:14.3: “Ajjatagge maṁ, bhante, bhagavā dāsīsamaṁ sāmikassa bhariyaṁ dhāretū”ti.

translation_text.an7.63:1.1: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went to the home of the householder Anāthapiṇḍika, where he sat on the seat spread out.
translation_text.an7.63:1.2: Now at that time people in Anāthapiṇḍika’s home were making a colossal racket.
translation_text.an7.63:1.3: Then the householder Anāthapiṇḍika went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him,
translation_text.an7.63:2.1: “Householder, what’s with the people making that colossal racket in your home? You’d think it was fishermen hauling in a catch!”
translation_text.an7.63:2.2: “Sir, that’s my daughter-in-law Sujātā. She’s been brought here from a wealthy family.
translation_text.an7.63:2.3: She doesn’t obey her mother-in-law or father-in-law or her husband. And she does not honor, respect, esteem, and venerate the Buddha.”
translation_text.an7.63:3.1: Then the Buddha addressed Sujātā, saying,
translation_text.an7.63:3.3: “Yes, sir,” she replied. She went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to her:
translation_text.an7.63:4.1: “Sujātā, a man can have seven kinds of wife.
translation_text.an7.63:4.3: A wife like a killer, a wife like a thief, a wife like a lord, a wife like a mother, a wife like a sister, a wife like a comrade, and a wife like a bondservant.
translation_text.an7.63:4.4: These are the kinds of wife that a man can have.
translation_text.an7.63:4.5: Which one of these are you?”
translation_text.an7.63:4.6: “Sir, I don’t understand the detailed meaning of what the Buddha has said in brief.
translation_text.an7.63:4.7: Please teach me this matter so I can understand the detailed meaning.”
translation_text.an7.63:4.8: “Well then, Sujātā, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an7.63:5.1: “With a mind full of hate and no kindness,
translation_text.an7.63:5.2: lusting for others, looking down on her husband,
translation_text.an7.63:5.3: she longs to murder him who paid the price for her.
translation_text.an7.63:5.4: A man’s wife of this sort
translation_text.an7.63:5.5: is called a wife and a killer.
translation_text.an7.63:6.1: A woman’s husband earns his wealth
translation_text.an7.63:6.2: by focusing on a profession, trade, or farming.
translation_text.an7.63:6.3: And even if it’s only a little, she wants to take it.
translation_text.an7.63:6.4: A man’s wife of this sort
translation_text.an7.63:6.5: is called a wife and a thief.
translation_text.an7.63:7.1: She’s an idle glutton who doesn’t want to work.
translation_text.an7.63:7.2: Her words are harsh, fierce, and rude.
translation_text.an7.63:7.3: He works hard, but she lives as his master.
translation_text.an7.63:7.4: A man’s wife of this sort
translation_text.an7.63:7.5: is called a wife and a lord.
translation_text.an7.63:8.2: looking after her husband like a mother her child.
translation_text.an7.63:8.3: She keeps the wealth that he has earned secure.
translation_text.an7.63:8.4: A man’s wife of this sort
translation_text.an7.63:8.5: is called a wife and a mother.
translation_text.an7.63:9.2: as a younger sister respects her elder.
translation_text.an7.63:9.3: Conscientious, she does what her husband says.
translation_text.an7.63:9.4: A man’s wife of this sort
translation_text.an7.63:9.5: is called a wife and a sister.
translation_text.an7.63:10.2: like one reunited with a long-lost comrade.
translation_text.an7.63:10.3: She’s of good pedigree, virtuous, and devoted.
translation_text.an7.63:10.4: A man’s wife of this sort
translation_text.an7.63:10.5: is called a wife and a comrade.
translation_text.an7.63:11.1: She has no anger <j>when threatened with violence by the rod.
translation_text.an7.63:11.3: she withstands her husband and does what he says.
translation_text.an7.63:11.4: A man’s wife of this sort
translation_text.an7.63:11.5: is called a wife and a bondservant.
translation_text.an7.63:12.1: The kinds of wives here called
translation_text.an7.63:12.3: immoral, harsh, and lacking regard for others,
translation_text.an7.63:12.4: when their body breaks up they set course for hell.
translation_text.an7.63:13.1: But the kinds of wives here called
translation_text.an7.63:13.3: steadfast in their own morality, <j>restrained for a long time,
translation_text.an7.63:13.4: when their body breaks up <j>they set course for a good place.
translation_text.an7.63:14.1: Sujātā, these are the seven kinds of wife that a man can have.
translation_text.an7.63:14.2: Which one of these are you?”
translation_text.an7.63:14.3: “Sir, from this day forth may the Buddha remember me as a wife like a bondservant.”
